seoTitle: How to Get a Slack QR Code for Mobile Sign-In
metaDescription: Generate a Slack QR code from your desktop app to sign into the mobile app instantly, without typing a password on your phone.
intro: Typing enterprise passwords and workspace URLs on a phone screen is tedious. This guide shows how to generate a Slack QR code from your desktop app and scan it with your phone for instant, secure sign-in.

steps.heading: Open your workspace profile
steps.body: Click your workspace name in the top left of the Slack desktop app to reveal the profile menu.

steps.heading: Access the sign-in option
steps.body: Hover over your organization profile panel and click 'Sign in on Mobile' to launch the code generator.

steps.heading: Display the QR code
steps.body: Keep the generated, single-use QR code visible on your desktop screen.

steps.heading: Open the Slack app on mobile
steps.body: Launch a freshly installed or existing Slack app on your phone and tap Sign In.

steps.heading: Scan to authenticate
steps.body: Select 'Scan QR Code', grant camera access, and align your phone with the desktop screen to complete sign-in.

faq.question: How long does a Slack QR code stay valid?
faq.answer: The code is single-use and expires after a short window for security reasons.

faq.question: Can I use the QR code to sign into more than one device?
faq.answer: You'll need to generate a new QR code for each additional device you want to sign in.

faq.question: What if my phone camera won't scan the code?
faq.answer: Make sure camera permissions are granted to the Slack app and that your screen brightness is high enough for a clear scan.

faq.question: Is QR sign-in safer than typing a password?
faq.answer: It avoids exposing your password on a small mobile keyboard and relies on an already authenticated desktop session.
